title: The tag with nothing to wrap
testCase: Did you add the image with and a correct path?
blocks.0.text:
Until now every tag of yours wrapped something.

text

.
  • text
    • . You open, put content in, close.
blocks.1.text: An image does not work that way. There is nothing to put inside it — it IS the content. So has no closing tag and nothing in between. Everything it says, it says in its attributes.
blocks.3.items.0: src — where to fetch the image from. The path you already know.
blocks.3.items.1: alt — what is on it, in words. The next lesson is about it, and it is not optional.
blocks.4.text:
You have met these —
and
were the same. Tags that hold nothing, they just do something on the spot.
blocks.5.text: Add the image below the paragraph and run. The file is /uroci/bike.jpg.
blocks.7.text: It shows up, but it takes over. It pushes the page down, maybe sideways too. This is not a bug: the image has its own size in pixels and the browser shows it as it is. It does not know how much room you have.
blocks.8.text: The temptation is to add width="400" and be done. It works. And it is a trap — the same one you have already seen twice.
blocks.9.text: The number 400 means nothing. Tomorrow the screen is 320 wide.
blocks.10.text: The real answer is CSS: max-width: 100% — "be yourself, but never wider than the room you have". One line, works on every screen, forever. We will get there.
blocks.13.text: Surprise: is not a block. It is inline — like and . That is why you can drop a small icon into the middle of a sentence without breaking the line. It is just that when the image is big, the line is as tall as the image, so you cannot tell.
blocks.14.text: Your turn. Add the image. The path is /uroci/bike.jpg — and what to write in alt you will find out in the next lesson. For now, write what you see.
